Perhaps a better comparison to the US is Europe. After all, overturning RvW has really just devolved the power of legislation on this issue back to individual state level. As I recall, different EC countries had different laws on abortion even back in the 80s, and people used regularly to have to travel in order to have them legally, which I imagine will now be paralleled by people in the US having to travel to another state.
